Jon: 	You know what? I left that part out on purpose, because I wanted
somebody to ask the question. I’m kidding.
I use 2 to 3 lies. You only want to do 2 to 3. If you go to long,
obviously, there’s so much negativity. If there are legitimate reasons
for more, like if you were to say, “Look there are 5 lies that people
believe,” then all you would do is shorten the nightmare story part of it.
Because you could just say, “One thing people believe is this. Well,
that’s not true, and if you do this, this will happen. And I don’t want
that for you. And another thing people believe is this.”
So, you can make those into literally 4 slides. You can condense this
all into 4 slides.
Jon: 	Let’s get some questions and answers on this section. If there’s no
questions, hopefully it means that it’s so clear and easy to understand
that you guys are going, “Man, okay, piece of cake. I can do that.” But

Okay, let’s get to the lies and the benefits. If someone doesn’t
understand or someone doesn’t know what lie they could use, I believe
somebody asked a while ago, “How do I know it’s the best lie?” Okay,
let’s get back to that, Josh.
Josh: 	Yeah, Amy had said that she is using having to talk for hours and
hours, what solves your relationship problems. And that was one of
the lies.
Jon: 	Okay, that’s awesome, Amy. And I’m going to walk right through the
lie formula here, the conspiracy cycle. And by the way, we’re getting to
the whole conspiracy cycle in the next section. So, if you’re
wondering, “Where did the conspiracy go?” well, this is where it gets to
be a lot of fun. Conspiracies are really, really cool to create. So, hang
with us, that’s in section part 2.
But let’s do your lie. Okay, so I would say, “You know, a lot of people
believe, in fact most people believe that you have to spend hours and
hours and hours talking to each other, in order to solve your
relationship problems. You know what? That’s just not true. And if
you do that you’re going to end up driving each other crazy. You’re
going to end up spending more time like working on your relationship,
rather than playing.”
And see, I can just go on and on here.
You just want to tell people what happens if they do that lie. And by
the way, just so you know, I think the vast majority of emails that I’ve
received were people saying, “I don’t know if this is good or not,” have
all been excellent. “I don’t know if my nightmare story is really good,”
it’s awesome. Again, you guys are much better than you think at this.
So, that is an awesome, awesome problem. You’re so close to your
product that you can’t see, sometimes, that you have distinctions like
that, that other people like me wouldn’t have. Like if I’m not really
savvy in relationships, I might believe that, “Oh, yeah to work on my
relationship is going to take hours and hours and I don’t have the time,
and we have kids,” or whatever, if you can break that down and say,
“You know what’s going to happen if you spend hours and hours doing
this? If you have children, you’re going to lose time with them. If your
guy isn’t a talker, it’s just going to be like pulling teeth.” I can just go on
and on here.
But yeah, that’s an excellent, excellent lie. So good one.
Josh: 	Alright, this is another kind of reinforcement from earlier. “How do you
tell them the lies that they believe without suggesting that you know
what they believe or think?” This is from Gina.

Jon: 	Okay, Gina, let’s go back over that in the actual PowerPoint file. I want
to skip back over here and I’ll show you exactly how to do that. That is
an excellent question, by the way. This is why we give you these.
Let’s reread my example of how I describe the lie.
Okay, I tell them what the real problem is. “The problem is you’ve
been lied to.” So you’re not assuming that they know anything. You’re
just saying they’ve been lied to. This is where I set up how I don’t have
to pretend like I can read their minds.
I say, “One of these lies is that you have to starve yourself skinny. And
I bet you’ve heard that, or maybe even believed this.” Those are the
keys. “I bet.” So I’m not saying, “I know you’ve heard that. “I bet
you’ve heard that, or maybe even believed it.” So that, right there,
encompasses every person on the planet.
Say, “Maybe you believed it.” “Well, no I didn’t believe it. Okay, that’s
cool. He said maybe.” So that’s all you have to do. Give me one of
your lies, and I’ll give you an example, off the top of my head. We’ll
walk into the describe the lie part using this kind of terminology. So,
type that in and Josh will read it off to me.

end it.
“A lot of marketers believe that they have to lie, cheat, and steal their
way to the top of Google. But, you know what? That’s not true. In
fact, when you do that, you’re going to end up getting in trouble.
You’re going to end up…” – you just go down the line of what happens

“And you deserve better. Your business deserves better.” You just let

not achieved the SEO rankings that you’ve wanted, it’s not your fault.
This isn’t your fault, because you believed these lies, like so many
other people do.”
And you’ll see, when we get into the next section, that I say this all the

-- 5 of 9 --

6
time. I say, “You know what? I believed this myself, for years.” And
that is doubling the rapport. So, if you can say something like that,
which again we’re getting into this in part 2. But when you can say, “I
believed that lie for years,” that really builds rapport.
If they’re listening to the expert who says, “I believed these lies for
years,” then it’s awesome. I’ll give you a perfect example of that with
me and my weight loss stuff.
It’s like for years, before I started studying nutrition, I believed that the
only way to lose weight was to do cardio 4 hours a day or to go on
some ridiculous starvation diet, or liquid diet, Hollywood diet, blah,
blah, blah. I believed that eating tons of carbs was healthy. I believed
all the lies.
So, when I say, “You may have believed this lie yourself,” and I’m like,
“Yeah, I believed it myself, so don’t feel so bad.” And when I do that, it
makes them go, “Oh, well if he believed it and he’s achieved this, then
I don’t feel so bad.” That’s really the goal here.
Again, if you just take your lies, whatever they are, and you follow this
formula, just follow the formula, go back. Again you’ve got the
downloads of the slides, you can rewatch the video. Follow the
formula. Once you do that, it will simply fall into place. Just write it as
we say that you should write it.
“So, one of the things you may believe is blank. This isn’t actually true.
And here’s the problem. If you believe blank, this is going to happen.
And this is going to happen. I don’t want that for you.” Does that
answer the question?

You just start off by simply saying, “You know what? A lot of people

you’re not falling all the time, you’re not trying. Well, guess what?
That’s not true, at all. In fact, if you take that strategy, you’re going to
end up getting hurt, you could end up getting seriously injured. You
could end up getting frustrated and quitting skiing altogether. And this
is not what I want for you.”

9
So you’d want to say like, “Make your car look like a brand new.” I
think you had the showroom stopper. “Without those nasty monthly
payments of having a new car.” So you just want to make sure that
that’s clear.
But, that is a great without. It’s an awesome without. So, in other
words, this is how you would go into that. That’s a big lie. “You know,
a lot of people believe that the only way to have a new-looking car is to
get a new car. That’s not true at all. In fact, if you go that route, good
luck, because you can be in financial debt for years. The economy is
blah.” You can paint this horrible picture.
You can say, “I know one guy.” And we’ll get into some advanced
techniques in the second section. “I know one guy that literally lost his
house because he had to have that new car. Lost his house over it.”
Actually, that’s a true story. Sad, but true.
So, you could paint a really vivid nightmare picture. “I don’t want that
for you, and you don’t need to do that. The truth is” – and we’re getting
to the truth in the next section – “you can make your car look brand
fricking new without having anything to do with monthly payments.”
